output_text:
{"covered_dims": {"R1.1": 0.6, "R1.2": 0.9, "R1.3": 1.0, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.5, "R1.6": 0.5, "R1.7": 0.6}}

Summary: Critique focused on a major evidence-claim mismatch — the experimental protocol described does not test the robustness claims the section makes — plus several quantitative and scoping issues.

Major issues

1. The headline claim is not supported by the experiments described. The section repeatedly frames the contribution as robustness to "heavy occlusion, sparse LiDAR returns, lossy V2V channels, or partial sensor failure," but the only reported evaluation is the **default OPV2V test split** with a clean simulated V2V channel. Nothing in the reported protocol perturbs channel reliability, drops agents, injects sensor noise, or stratifies by occlusion severity. Either (a) add experiments that actually exercise these conditions (e.g., packet-loss sweeps, agent-dropout, fog/rain or point-dropout corruptions, occlusion-stratified subsets) and report results on them, or (b) drop the robustness framing and reposition the contribution as "small accuracy gains on clean OPV2V." As written, the conclusion ("leading approach for robust cooperative perception under sensor degradation, occlusion, and communication unreliability") is unearned.

2. The magnitude of the gain does not justify the strength of the conclusion. +0.5 mAP@0.5 and +0.8 mAP@0.7 over CoBEVT on a single benchmark, with no variance reported across seeds/runs and no statistical test, is within the noise band typically seen on OPV2V. Add (i) mean ± std over ≥3 seeds, (ii) a significance test or confidence interval, and (iii) ideally results on a second dataset (V2X-Sim, DAIR-V2X) before claiming SOTA.

3. The PointPillars comparison is mis-scoped. Calling "PointPillars with no fusion" the "strongest single-agent baseline" and then reporting a 1.6 mAP@0.5 gap is a comparison against a non-cooperative model — it speaks to the value of cooperation in general, not to MARCO's contribution. The meaningful single-agent baseline is the best non-cooperative detector on OPV2V (e.g., SECOND, CenterPoint, PV-RCNN); state which one and why. Better: separate "value of any fusion" from "value of selective fusion" by also reporting against simple late/early/intermediate fusion baselines.

4. Ablation is under-powered. A single 0.4 mAP@0.5 drop from removing the gate "confirms its role" only weakly, especially when the total gain over CoBEVT is 0.5 mAP@0.5 — meaning the gate accounts for nearly the entire delta and almost nothing else in MARCO matters. Either lean into this (the gate **is** the contribution) and ablate it thoroughly (gating variants, gate input features, per-cell vs per-agent gating, behavior under induced corruption) or acknowledge that the rest of the architecture contributes little.

5. The backbone-generalization paragraph asserts results without showing them. "The overall mAP gains hold under both [PointPillars and SECOND]" needs numbers in a table; otherwise this claim should be cut.

Smaller issues

- The OPV2V protocol description omits training details (epochs, optimizer, communication bandwidth budget, BEV resolution, compression scheme) that readers need to reproduce or contextualize the numbers.
- "2-7 connected vehicles" — confirm this matches the actual OPV2V distribution; some prior work cites 2–5.
- "Aggregated across all scenes" is ambiguous: per-frame mean or per-scene mean? Specify.
- The closing sentence of paragraph 4 ("establish MARCO as the leading approach...") and the opening sentence of paragraph 5 ("also architecturally compatible...") should be merged or reordered; the current ordering puts the strongest claim before its qualifying evidence.
- "Selective feature-fusion module" and "selective fusion module" and "selective gate" are used interchangeably — pick one term.
- "Lossy V2V channels" appears in the framing but the simulated channel is described as just "a simulated V2V channel" without loss characteristics. Specify the channel model.

Suggested reframe of the opening sentence
Current: "...demonstrate that inter-agent feature sharing yields strong robustness to real-world sensor degradation."
Replace with: "...and report detection accuracy on the standard test split; robustness to channel and sensor degradation is examined in Section X." (Then either deliver Section X or remove the robustness framing throughout.)

Uncertainty disclosure: I have not verified the published CoBEVT numbers (88.7 / 80.6) against the original paper, nor confirmed the current OPV2V leaderboard state as of 2026; the "leading approach" claim should be checked against recent V2X-Sim / CoBEVT-v2 / HEAL / CoAlign results before publication.
